---
title: "exp15_FINAL"
author: "Maya Śliwa"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
setwd("~/Desktop/Szade_Lab/aktualne")
```

```{r}
cyto_cols <- c(
  "all_events",
  "singlets_cells",
  "Live_dead_Ly6C_subset",
  "cKit_neg",
  "low6c_mono",
  "monocytes_1",
  "neutrophils",
  "non_mielo",
  "FcR_neg",
  "CD150neg_CD105pos",
  "CD150pos_CD105pos",
  "CD150pos_CD105neg",
  "CD150neg_CD105neg",
  "cKit_pos",
  "GMP",
  "non_GMP",
  "CFU_E",
  "CMP",
  "preCFUE",
  "preGM",
  "preMegE",
  "trombo"
)

# wczytanie danych
df <- read_excel("ERC_aim1_exp15_summary.xlsx") 

# OBIEKT R z samą cytometrią
df_cyto <- df %>%
  select(all_of(cyto_cols))

# normalizacja % of live dla wszystkich
denom <- "Live_dead_Ly6C_subset"
gate_cols <- setdiff(colnames(df_cyto), denom)

df_cyto_pct_live <- df_cyto %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(gate_cols),
      ~ ifelse(.data[[denom]] == 0 | is.na(.data[[denom]]), NA_real_, (.x / .data[[denom]]) * 100),
      .names = "{.col}_pct_live"
    )
  )

#normalizacja % of live dla zgatowanych populacji
gated_populations <- c(
  "cKit_neg",
  "low6c_mono",
  "monocytes_1",
  "neutrophils",
  "non_mielo",
  "FcR_neg",
  "CD150neg_CD105pos",
  "CD150pos_CD105pos",
  "CD150pos_CD105neg",
  "CD150neg_CD105neg",
  "cKit_pos",
  "GMP",
  "non_GMP",
  "CFU_E",
  "CMP",
  "preCFUE",
  "preGM",
  "preMegE",
  "trombo"
)

denom <- "Live_dead_Ly6C_subset"
df_gated_pct_live <- df_cyto %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(gated_populations),
      ~ ifelse(
        .data[[denom]] == 0 | is.na(.data[[denom]]),
        NA_real_,
        (.x / .data[[denom]]) * 100
      ),
      .names = "{.col}_pct_live"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(ends_with("_pct_live"))
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

# 1. NA -> 0
pca_df <- df_gated_pct_live %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ replace_na(.x, 0)))

# usuwamy kolumny o zerowej wariancji
zero_var_cols <- pca_df %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ var(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "col", values_to = "var") %>%
  filter(is.na(var) | var == 0) %>%
  pull(col)

zero_var_cols

pca_df_filtered <- pca_df %>%
  select(-all_of(zero_var_cols))

# macierz do PCA
pca_mat <- as.matrix(pca_df_filtered)

# PCA bez log-transform
pca_res <- prcomp(
  pca_mat,
  center = TRUE,
  scale. = TRUE
)

# procent wariancji wyjaśnionej
var_explained <- (pca_res$sdev^2) / sum(pca_res$sdev^2)
var_explained_percent <- var_explained * 100

var_explained_percent
cumsum(var_explained_percent)
```

Bierzemy pierwsze 6 PC\
PC6 → 80.70%

```{r}
# 3. bierzemy pierwsze 6 PC
pca_data <- pca_res$x[, 1:6, drop = FALSE]
```

```{r}
library(uwot)
# UMAP
umap_res <- uwot::umap(pca_data)

# do ramki danych
umap_df <- data.frame(
  UMAP1 = umap_res[,1],
  UMAP2 = umap_res[,2]
)

head(umap_df)
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(umap_df, aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Chcę zrobić teraz DBSCAN i wybrać odpowiedni parametr eps, więc zastosuje metodę elbow:

```{r}
library(dbscan)

kNNdistplot(umap_res, k = 5)
abline(h = 0.5, col = "red", lty = 2)
```

```{r}
library(dbscan)

db <- dbscan(umap_res, eps = 0.35, minPts = 5) # wybrane z wykresu elbow powyżej!!!

umap_df$cluster <- as.factor(db$cluster)

ggplot(umap_df, aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal()
```
